entry.title: All NASA News - NASA
entry.summary: Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.

entry.title: NASA Media Contacts - NASA
entry.summary: Media Contacts Gerelle DodsonNASA Workforce, STEM Engagement202-358-1600Jennifer DoorenDeputy News Chief, Cybersecurity202-358-1600Tiernan Doyle

entry.title: NASA News
entry.summary: Resources for news media, including NASA news contacts and archived news material

entry.title: NASA, JAXA to Cover HTV-X1 Spacecraft Departure from Space Station - NASA
entry.summary: On Thursday, March 5, flight controllers will use the space station’s Canadarm2 robotic arm to detach HTV-X1 from the Harmony module’s Earth-facing port on

entry.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
entry.summary: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

entry.title: Japan’s Cargo Spacecraft Nears Departure as Crew Works Spacewalk Preps, Science - NASA
entry.summary: Mission managers have given the go for a Japanese cargo spacecraft to depart the International Space Station at the end of the week. Meanwhile, the Expedition 74 crew is reviewing upcoming spacewalk procedures, packing a second cargo spacecraft for its departure, studying artificial intelligence tools, and conducting Earth observations.

entry.title: Spacewalk and Japanese Cargo Craft Departure Preps Kick Off Week - NASA
entry.summary: The Expedition 74 crew kicked off the work week readying a spacesuit and studying procedures for an upcoming spacewalk later this month. The International Space Station residents are also packing a Japanese cargo craft and preparing it for departure from the International Space Station at the end of the week.

entry.title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Sees Martian ‘Spiderwebs’ Up Close
entry.summary: A hilly landscape that looks like spiderwebs when viewed from orbit holds clues to the history of water on ancient Mars.

entry.title: NASA’s Perseverance Now Autonomously Pinpoints Its Location on Mars
entry.summary: Using the new Mars Global Localization algorithm, NASA’s Perseverance rover can now pinpoint its exact location without help from engineers on Earth.

entry.title: Archival Data From NASA’s NEOWISE Tracks Star Turning Into Black Hole
entry.summary: Using archival data from the NEOWISE mission, astronomers have captured the rare moment a massive star skipped the supernova phase and collapsed directly into a black hole.

entry.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Completes First AI-Planned Drive on Mars
entry.summary: In a first for planetary exploration, NASA utilized generative AI to map routes for the Perseverance rover, cutting planning time and increasing science returns.

entry.title: NASA-ISRO Radar Mission Peers Through Clouds to See Mississippi River Delta
entry.summary: NASA’s NISAR mission reveals new details of the Mississippi River Delta’s forests and wetlands, proving radar’s power to monitor ecosystems regardless of weather.

entry.title: NASA Analysis Shows La Niña Limited Sea Level Rise in 2025
entry.summary: A mild La Niña caused greater rainfall over the Amazon basin, which offset rising sea levels due to record warming of Earth’s oceans.

entry.title: Networks Keeping NASA’s Artemis II Mission Connected
entry.summary: Artemis missions rely on both the Near Space Network, managed by NASA Goddard, and the Deep Space Network, managed by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

entry.title: NASA’s Juno Measures Thickness of Europa’s Ice Shell
entry.summary: Results from the solar-powered spacecraft provide a new measurement of the thickness of the ice shell encasing the Jovian moon’s ocean.

entry.title: NASA, SpaceX Launch US-European Satellite to Monitor Earth’s Oceans
entry.summary: Sentinel-6B will extend a decades-long legacy of global sea level measurements that help forecast weather, protect coastal communities, and more.

entry.title: News
entry.summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, the leading center for robotic exploration of the solar system.

entry.title: News | Rocket Lab
entry.summary: Keep up with the latest from Rocket Lab.

entry.title: 6 Reasons A Mars Telecommunications Orbiter Matters To Us All | Rocket Lab
entry.summary: 6 Reasons A Mars Telecommunications Orbiter Matters To Us All

entry.title: Mission Success: Rocket Lab Launches 2nd Hypersonic Test Mission in Three Months for Defense Innovation Unit | Rocket Lab
entry.summary: Long Beach, Calif. February 27: Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) (“Rocket Lab” or “the Company”), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today successfully launched its latest hypersonic test mission on its HASTE rocket for the Department of War’s Defense Innovation Unit (DIU…

entry.title: Rocket Lab Completes Spacecraft Commissioning for NASA’s ESCAPADE Mars Mission | Rocket Lab
entry.summary: Long Beach, Calif. February 26, 2026 – Rocket Lab USA, a wholly owned subsidiary of Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) (“Rocket Lab” or “the Company”), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced the successful completion of commissioning for the twin Rocket Lab-built…

entry.title: Rocket Lab Introduces Advanced Silicon Solar Arrays To Power Space-Based Data Centers | Rocket Lab
entry.summary:
As surging AI and compute demand pushes terrestrial data centers to their limit, space-based data centers can harness infinitely abundant solar energy in orbit thanks to Rocket Lab’s rapidly scalable silicon solar arrays 
 
Long Beach, Calif. – February 26, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq…

entry.title: Rocket Lab Acquires Optical Support, Inc., Strengthening National Security Payload Capability | Rocket Lab
entry.summary: Long Beach, California – February 26, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) (“Rocket Lab” or the “Company”), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced it has completed the acquisition of Optical Support, Inc. (OSI), a leader in the design, engineering, and…

entry.title: January 2026 Company Update
entry.summary: January marked a strong start to the year, with meaningful progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released 1,856 flight parts. At this stage of the program, part releases are primarily for secondary structures and integration hardware that will be installed l

entry.title: December 2025 Company Update
entry.summary: As we start the new year, here’s a look at key Terran R program milestones from December 2025.  Design This month, the team released 1,526 flight parts and the integrated thrust structure fluids assembly, unlocking downstream manufacturing and keeping the integration schedule moving at p

entry.title: November 2025 Company Update
entry.summary: Throughout November, the Terran R program made key advancements across design, build, test, and launch operations. Design As the program continues to mature, we are releasing larger groups of parts through our design sprints. In November, we released 1,602 parts, unlocking downstream manufacturin

entry.title: SES, Relativity Space Expand Multi-Launch Agreement for Terran R
entry.summary: New contract adds launch services for SES’s GEO or MEO satellites
